title: Government Lists — source registry
description: Phonebook of where to pull each government list type. Collect asks cities; this page remembers the sources.

listTypes.label: Pre-liens
listTypes.summary: Civil/small-claims complaints before judgment — 2–3 month edge vs recorded liens.

listTypes.label: Code violations
listTypes.summary: Open/unresolved code, nuisance, boarded, weeds — city code enforcement.

listTypes.label: Tax delinquent
listTypes.summary: Unpaid taxes / tax sale rolls — primary for vacant land.

listTypes.label: Pre-foreclosure (LP / NOD)
listTypes.summary: Lis pendens, notice of default, notice of sale — ask by document type, not “pre-foreclosure list.”

listTypes.label: Probate / estate
listTypes.summary: New estate filings with real property — live dockets beat vendor inheritance lists.

listTypes.label: Fire-damaged
listTypes.summary: Fire dept incident addresses — low competition if obtained.

listTypes.label: Evictions
listTypes.summary: Unlawful detainer dockets — outreach to landlord (plaintiff), not tenant.

listTypes.label: Water shutoffs
listTypes.summary: Non-payment shutoffs — high denial rate; try once per market, don’t build on it.

sources.notes: How-to: Civil/small-claims complaints before judgment — 2–3 month edge vs recorded liens. Custodian is usually the county. Use Collect for city code/water requests you already track.
sources.requestTemplate: Under the [STATE PUBLIC RECORDS ACT], I request access to newly filed civil and small-claims complaints (pre-judgment) for [COUNTY] from [START] to [END]. I need case search URLs or exports including case number, filing date, parties, and complaint/summons PDFs when available.

sources.notes: How-to: Open/unresolved code, nuisance, boarded, weeds — city code enforcement. Custodian is usually the city. Use Collect for city code/water requests you already track.
sources.requestTemplate: Under the [STATE PUBLIC RECORDS ACT], I request an electronic list of all open/unresolved code-violation cases for [CITY] from [START] to [END]. Please include property address, parcel ID, owner name, mailing address, case number, violation type, opened date, status, and compliance deadline when available. CSV or Excel preferred.

sources.notes:
Form type: Email Only · Pathway: email_only · no form just an email
Reference: https://www.cityofcenterpoint.org/198/Code-Enforcement

sources.notes: How-to: Unpaid taxes / tax sale rolls — primary for vacant land. Custodian is usually the county. Use Collect for city code/water requests you already track.
sources.requestTemplate: Under the [STATE PUBLIC RECORDS ACT], I request the current tax delinquent / tax-due roll for [COUNTY] (not only the next auction list). Please include parcel ID, property address, owner name, mailing address, years delinquent, amount owed, and any upcoming sale date. CSV or Excel preferred.

sources.notes: How-to: Lis pendens, notice of default, notice of sale — ask by document type, not “pre-foreclosure list.” Custodian is usually the county. Use Collect for city code/water requests you already track.
sources.requestTemplate: Please point me to official records search for lis pendens / notice of default / notice of trustee sale / bank foreclosure suits in [COUNTY] from [START] to [END]. I need document type filters and how to export or copy party + property info.

sources.notes: County Acclaim official records.

sources.notes: How-to: New estate filings with real property — live dockets beat vendor inheritance lists. Custodian is usually the county. Use Collect for city code/water requests you already track.
sources.requestTemplate: Under the [STATE PUBLIC RECORDS ACT], I request new probate / estate filings in [COUNTY] from [START] to [END], including decedent name, petitioner/personal representative, case number, filing date, and any real property listed.

sources.notes: How-to: Fire dept incident addresses — low competition if obtained. Custodian is usually the city. Use Collect for city code/water requests you already track.
sources.requestTemplate: I request an electronic list of residential fire incident addresses in [CITY/COUNTY] for the last 30–60 days (address and incident date). CSV or Excel preferred.

sources.notes: How-to: Unlawful detainer dockets — outreach to landlord (plaintiff), not tenant. Custodian is usually the county. Use Collect for city code/water requests you already track.
sources.requestTemplate: I request access to unlawful detainer / eviction filings in [COUNTY] from [START] to [END], including plaintiff (landlord), property address, case number, and filing date when available.

sources.notes: How-to: Non-payment shutoffs — high denial rate; try once per market, don’t build on it. Custodian is usually the city. Use Collect for city code/water requests you already track.
